feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Error in group provisioning to commerce cloud
entry.content:
Hello All,

Error occurring in Commerce cloud group provisioning .

User able to create bit fails in group provisioning.

Please suggest

Thanks

entry.title: IAS application:Subject Name Identifier
entry.content:
Hi All,

Please suggest the meaning of Source in IAS for an application, in 'Subject Name Identifier' .

regards

Plaban

entry.title: IAS applications: SAML 2.0
entry.content:
Hi All,

For IAS applications, Is SAML 2.0 planned to be deprecated

Regards Plaban

entry.title: Possible to replicate deprecated oAuth SF authentication using SAP Cloud Identity to SuccessFactors?
entry.content:
With the deprecation of OAuth IdP API in SuccessFactors, I'm looking to see if I can do the same thing with SAP Cloud Identity Services.

In short, SuccessFactors has a Todo api that I can use to get the total Todo's that I can display in the Fiori Launchpad but this API is user specific (cannot be executed by a dedicated API user). There is another v2 API that allows filtering by user but it does not work for all Todo types so not an option.

So using the Todo API was fine with the deprecated OAuth as it allowed me, via client id/secret to get a SAML token for any employee to call this API. 

So first question - Is this possible within SAP Cloud Identity?

Second question - I found this link discussed something similar but I'm wondering do I just need a single SAML2 Application created that can give me an access token, which I can convert to SAML2 to authenticate to SuccessFactors?

Lastly, how do I specify the user within the client request without password and get the SAML token to use for SuccessFactors?

e.g. Firstly, I posted to /oauth2/token with grant_type: client_credentials and client_id: "User id I want to log in as"? - with Basic Auth using client id and secret - but this doesn't seem to check the user-id, so pretty sure I'm doing this first step wrong?

If it is working, then I need to convert this to SAML2 via grant_type: urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange...

A little lost, so appreciate any help.

Thanks,
Matt

entry.title: How to update the Passord Policy of IAS users via REST API
entry.content:
Need to update the password policy only of IAS user because they are having trouble when logging in.

I have search and found that there is an API for that, but i would like to have an example. I mean to this API reference: https://api.sap.com/api/IdDS_SCIM/path/patchUser 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: How to force reset the password of user in IAS via REST Api
entry.content:
Hi guys.

There is a need to force reset the user's password in IAS via REST Api because actually they are not getting the correct Password Policy.

I mean to send a flag or update the user info in order to force a password reset next time the user tries to login.

Is there any way to do that?

entry.title: Enabling Access to SAP UI5 Applications for Non-BTP Users
entry.content:
I have developed an SAP UI5 application in Business Application Studio (BAS) and deployed it to Cloud Foundry. The application is now visible under HTML5 Applications in the SAP BTP Cockpit. I would like to understand how users without an SAP BTP account can access this application. Specifically, is it possible for users created in SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) to access this UI5 application?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Change the IAS tenant integrated with Learning
entry.content:
We have an IAS tenant integrated with two Learning instances and the goal is to change one of the connected SF and Learning pairs to a different IAS instance.

According to offical SAP sources (3447162 - Change the IAS tenant integrated with Learning - Learning&IAS pairing - SAP for Me) it is not possible to re-run the IAS Upgrade for Learning but I am wondering if there is a workaround or a way to achieve this by manually performing the configuration (create IAS applications ,define endpoints, etc.)

Thanks for any help or ideas.

Kind Regards,

Andreas

entry.title: Can I extend the self-registration for SAP Cloud Identity Services
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I want to enable the self-registration for an application registered in our SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant. Activating the self-registration and adding some information available in "User Attributes for Access" is fine and working.

But, we also want the user to enter the "Login name" (which we use as SSO attribute). Unfortunately, we cannot add this to the self-registration form. As we only allow internal users we could probably call an API to determine the Login name based on the email address as this would be available in a central repository.

How can ensure that the login name is set on self registration (either by entering in the according form or by some logic) before the first real login of the user?

entry.title: Assignement abap user roles by Cloud Identity Services from Entra Azure
entry.content:
Hello,

I need some help with Cloud Identity Services (CIS) : I need to manage the user in ABAP environnement (ECC and S/4) from Entra Azure.

So I've managed to create and update a User with certain information. But I also want to manage role assignement. To simplify the process I'm using the group assignement in Azure and use the description field to write the role I want to add to the users. The role is an existing role on the ABAP environnement.

I've seen that we can get the list of the groups for each user from the source system (Entra) - But I don't know if we can and How to use it to assign a role on the target system (SAP ECC and/or S/4 ).

Thanks in advance,

Regards,

Matthias

entry.title: User Attributes Not Transferred to SAP BTP from Azure AD via IAS with OIDC
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured SAP IAS as a proxy and Azure as IdP, with OIDC as protocol between Azure and IAS. I am trying to log  in BAS. but I have encountered an issue- Access denied.  The User is created in SAP BTP but the user name are not transferred correctly und first und last name are not transferred at all to SAP BTP:

Configuration in IAS:

  • Subject Name Identifier: (Corporate Identity Provider) - NameID

     

  • Attributes:

     

     

Configuration in Azure:

  • The necessary claims such as are available in Azure AD.

Where could the issue be in the configuration?

Thank you in advance for your help.

Best regards.

entry.title: CIS (Cloud Identity Services) - IAS & IPS
entry.content: New to CIS (Cloud Identity Services). My understanding is that both IAS (Identity Authentication Service) and IPS (Identity Provisioning Service) are available in CIS. Is it possible to use only the IPS portion of CIS or do you have to make use of both IPS and IAS (for authentication) when using CIS?

entry.title: IPS Error with SCIM 2
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am provisioning users from Azure to IAS, which works well so far. After that, I provision the users to SAC, but I encounter the following error:

user:34asa-34df-4434f-435f-5fgfgsdfgsfdgsdg,

system=Target - SAC

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://sac-tenant.com/api/v1/scim2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"mutability","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema."},

 content={"active":true,"displayName":"sacfirst, saclast","emails":[{"primary":true,"value":"sacfirst.saclast@domain.com"}],"groups":[{"value":"438b6f2a-5ec4-4d93-b9f6-13cb4ee9502d"}],"name":{"familyName":"saclast","givenName":"sacfirst"},"schemas":["urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User"],"urn:sap:params:scim:schemas:extension:sac:2.0:user-custom-parameters":{"idpUserId":"sacfirst.saclast@domain.com"},"userName":"sacfirst.saclast@domain.com"}

When I provision the users to IAS, the login name is set as the email address in IAS.

After that, the users are provisioned from IAS to SAC, and the error mentioned above occurs. We found that the error occurs because the login name from IAS (the email address) is transferred to the "USER ID" field in SAC. However, SAC does not allow the "USER ID" to be an email address.

This error happens with  SAP Analytics Cloud SCIM API version 2:

IAS Properties - sac.api.version=2, csrf.token.path=/api/v1/scim2/Users?count=1.

I copied the transformation code from the SAP help page for the target system - SAC.

How can this issue be resolved?

Thank you for your time and assistance.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: SAP Mobile Start fails to load App
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have created a site on Workzone where I have associated an app developed with SAP Build. If I open the site from the browser, I can access the app without any problems. However, if I access it via Mobile Start, I see the application tile, but when I click on it, I get the following error: "unable to load external app" on iOS, "unable to open app" on Android.

The application was developed using a trial account.

Below are all the steps I followed for the configuration:

  • Built and deployed the app in "SAP Build Apps | Build Service" for Work Zone
  • Added the app to Work Zone
  • Enabled the site setting for Mobile Start
  • Tested the app in the web launchpad and it works correctly
  • Downloaded Mobile Start on iOS and Android
  • Performed BTP authentication
  • Successfully connected to the launchpad
  • Viewed group and app
  • Clicking on the app, the error "unable to load external app" appears on iOS and "unable to open app" on Android

Do you have any idea what the problem might be?

Thank you all.

entry.title: Azure, IAS, and Cloud Apps Authentication
entry.content:
Hi All, Hi @Matthew_Shaw  

@Matthew_Shaw thank you so much for your help!

Now a clarification question: We have Azure as IdP, IAS as a proxy, and cloud apps like SAC configured. We provision users from Azure to IAS and then to SAC.

Ideally, we would like to log in to the apps  (like SAC) using an employee number (e.g., 283268).

I can of course provision the employee number (283268) from Azure to IAS in the "Login Name" field and then to SAP SAC in the "USER ID". However, when a user tries to log in to SAC, they cannot log in with the employee number (283268). This user is not found in Azure. If the user logs in with the email address, the login works. What am I doing wrong here?

Question 1: Is it possible to authenticate in cloud apps (such as SAC) using an employee number from Azure (since users are redirected to Azure for authentication)?

Question 2: In Azure, the UPN is set as the email address, e.g., firstname.lastname@mail.com. If I form the username "f.lastname" from firstname.lastname@mail.com and transfer it from Azure -> IAS -> SAC, can the user log in with "f.lastname"? Will this user be found in Azure with "f.lastname"? What is the best attribute from Azure to provision to the USER ID for authentication?

Thank you again. Unfortunately, I am stuck here and need help.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Cloud identity service user created failed login to an application
entry.content:
After following the guide (https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/create-openid-connect-application) and having established a trust connection to one of my applications in cloud identity service  , creating a new user and assigning it to a group of type (usergroup and creating the respective rule in Conditional Authentication) for my application, when I try to login via default identity provider I get "
Sorry, we could not authenticate you. Try again."

Please note that with an admin account created I cannot  log in.

I also tried without creating the group but nothing changes.

How can I resolve this issue?

thanks,

Davide

entry.title: Call rest api cloud identity service authentication
entry.content:
Hi experts, after successfully configuring my cloud identity service, establishing trust from my subaccount and then creating the application, I would like to be able to interact with it via api calls from an external system.

So I created an admin as system generated a secret.

So I started to get the first token with this api  https://xxxxxxx.accounts.ondemand.com/oauth2/token giving the client_id and client_secret in the body. The response give me a access_token.

When I try to consume the api using the access_token as bearer in my request I receive unauthorized.

What I missing?  I have to configure more?  thanks

entry.title: IPS Provisioning to and from non-SAP 3rd part application
entry.content:
Good day, 

Is it possible to provision to and/or from a non-SAP 3rd party application with IPS? From what I read this might be possible but looking at IPS itself there is no option for any 'unknown' or 'non-SAP solution' systems in the drop down like there is for creating new applications in IAS.

Is this possible? And if it is, is there a blog or tutorial somewhere for guidance on how to connect IPS to 3rd party systems?

Below are the screenshots on IPS vs IAS for non-SAP systems:

IPS target & source systems:

IAS applications:

Thanks

Vedette

entry.title: Two applications for SAP Build Work Zone Standard in IAS
entry.content:
Dear Gurus

I have published SAP Build Work Zone standart in BTP trial subaccount.

Now in SAP Cloud Identity service I can see two applications.

What is the purpose of second application?

Which application I need to select from user provisioning?

Thank you

Regards

Vladimir Kogan

entry.title: Fetch user details (country) from IAS in the approuter
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

I am working on a Cloud Foundry Approuter in SAP BTP and have the following custom middleware code:

 

const approuter = require('@sap/approuter');

const ar = approuter();

ar.beforeRequestHandler.use('/', function (req, res, next) {
    if (req.url.includes('/sap/opu/odata/MY_SERVICE/')) {
        req.headers.email_user = req.user.name;
        console.log("username");
        console.log(req.user.name);
        console.log("headers");
        console.log(req.headers);
        console.log("req");
        console.log(req);
    }
    
    next();
});

ar.start();

 

 

In this setup, I am trying to retrieve the country of the authenticated user. I know the user's email is accessible via req.user.name and I have also found the JWT Access Token in the request. However, the token does not include any region or country information.

Since country information is maintained in SAP IAS (Identity Authentication Service), I’m wondering:

  • Is it possible to retrieve the country of the user from the req object or its associated JWT token?
  • If not, what would be the recommended approach to fetch country information from IAS in an SAP BTP App Router application?

Any pointers or advice would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!
Mohammed
